title: NOC 51121 – Conductors, Composers and Arrangers | GTR Immigration
description: Conductors, Composers, and Arrangers under NOC 51121 are creative music professionals responsible for leading performances, composing original music, and adapting musical works across Canada’s arts and entertainment industries. Classified under TEER 1, this occupation typically requires formal music education and demonstrated artistic talent. These professionals are commonly employed by:

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category5 – Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sportTEER1 – Occupations usually require a university degreeMajor Group51 – Professional occupations in art and cultureSub-Major Group511 – Professional occupations in art and cultureMinor Group5112 – Creative and performing artistsNOC Code51121

duties: Lead orchestras, bands, and choirs during rehearsals and performances
Guide musicians to achieve artistic excellence
Create original music for films, television, games, and performances
Compose symphonies, songs, and theme music
Tailor music to suit specific vocal or instrumental performances
Arrange works to achieve desired stylistic or thematic effects
employment_requirements: A university degree in music or completion of a program at a conservatory or music academy is usually required
Specialized study in conducting, composing, or arranging is typically required
Experience as a musician is required
A portfolio demonstrating musical and creative talent is an important hiring criterion
salary_prospects: Conductors, Composers, and Arrangers typically earn:
$27,500 – $88,100 per year, depending on experience, specialization, and performance scale.
With 15,700 projected job openings and 13,300 job seekers, NOC 51121 reflects favorable growth potential in Canada’s music and entertainment industries.
Growth in streaming platforms and digital music
Opportunities are strongest in provinces with active arts industries such as Ontario and British Columbia.
NOC 51121 is classified under TEER 1, making it generally eligible under:
Eligibility depends on education credential assessment (ECA), language proficiency, and relevant skilled work experience.

Guide you through documentation and credential evaluation

labour_market_demand: Growth of Canada’s music and entertainment industries
Expansion of digital streaming and multimedia productions
Film, television, and gaming soundtrack production
Replacement demand in orchestras and performing arts
With more projected job openings than job seekers, employment prospects are favorable.
Opportunities in live performance and digital production
Creative careers in film, gaming, and broadcast industries
Music professionals under this NOC contribute significantly to Canada’s cultural and creative economy.
